race.name: GFNY Cannes
race.tagline: Be a Pro for a Day on the French Riviera

vitals.field_size: Sold out in inaugural year; controlled entry
vitals.registration: Online via official website; age 18+, medical documentation required for competitive category

climate.description: Mild Mediterranean spring conditions; early morning climbs may be cold and shaded with potential dew on descents
challenges: early morning climbs may be cold and shaded with potential dew on descents

terrain.surface: Hilly road loop through Provencal villages and preserved hinterland; key climbs include Col de Tanneron (7.5-7.6km at 4.5-5%) and Col d'Avaye (13.7km at 4.1%)
features: Hilly road loop through Provencal villages and preserved hinterland
key climbs include Col de Tanneron (7

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 77.

course_description.character: 110km loop leaves coastal glamour for preserved hinterland. **Suffering Zones:** 1) Col de Tanneron (7.5km @4.5%, km11-18: cold, shaded north face with slippery descent) 2) 13% kicker (300m @38.6km) 3) Col d'Avaye (13.7km @4.1%, km45+: two-part climb) 4) Final 2km double climb (km90-92) 5) 3km-to-go steep ramp for winning attacks. Rewarded by Lac de Saint-Cassien views and seaside finish[1][4][5]

logistics.transport: Start/finish at Plage de Cannes La Bocca along Boulevard du Midi. Race week expo and activities at Palais des Festivals and Gare Maritime. Nice Côte d'Azur Airport (NCE) primary access. Registration requires age 18+ verification and medical documentation for competitive long course[1][2][3][4]

history.origin_story: Inaugural event March 27, 2022 sold out and opened European GFNY season. Part of six French GFNY events that year. 2025 edition earned FFC TROPHÉE LABEL D'OR quality certification. 2026 moves to April 12[1][3][4]

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Cannes perfectly blends Riviera glamour with authentic gran fondo suffering. The cold Tanneron climb wakes you up early season while Avaye's two-part ascent tests your form. Post-race recovery options from Picasso Museum to Gorges du Verdon make this more than just a bike race—it's a Côte d'Azur lifestyle event. Top 10% age-group finishers earn qualifier corral privileges across the GFNY World Series.

final_verdict.one_liner: Elite season opener combining professional racing structure with French Riviera spectacle. T1 status earned through adventure, experience, and GFNY pedigree.
